name: Cannoli Alla Siciliana
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Orange peel, candied and chopped
recipeInstructions:
To make cannoli shells, you will need 3 or 4 metal tubes, preferably made from very light tin, about 7 inches long and 1 1/8 inches in diameter. The edges should not be soldered (old aluminum chairs cut into pieces work well).
1. Combine flour, shortening, sugar, and salt, gradually wetting with wine until a dough forms.
2. Form the dough into a ball, cover with cloth, and let stand for about 1 hour.
3. Cut the dough in half and roll out one portion into a thin sheet (about 1/4 inch thick).
4. Cut the sheet into 4-inch squares.
5. Place a metal tube diagonally across each square from one point to another, wrapping the dough around the tube by overlapping points and sealing the overlaps with a little egg white.
6. Meanwhile, heat vegetable oil in a large deep pan for frying.
7. Drop one or two tubes at a time into hot oil and fry gently until golden brown.
8. Remove from the pan, let cool, and gently remove the shell from the metal tube.
9. Repeat until all shells are made.
Filling:
1. Mix ricotta thoroughly with sifted dry ingredients (cinnamon, grated chocolate, or cocoa).
2. Add vanilla and fruit peel; mix well.
3. Chill in the refrigerator before filling shells.
4. Fill cold cannoli shells, smoothing the filling evenly at each end of the shell.
5. Decorate each end with a piece of glace cherry and sprinkle with confectioners' sugar.
6. Refrigerate until ready to serve. Best if filled just before guests arrive.
From: Polly-O Cooking with Cheese Recipe Book
